mnemonics: P.R.O.T.O.T.Y.P.E: Plan, Refine, Organize, Test, Optimize, Then Your Product Excels
keyPoints: Prototypes are early, inexpensive models used to test and validate design concepts before development.
Fidelity (low to high) refers to the level of detail and functionality a prototype possesses.
The core purpose is to gather feedback, identify usability issues, and align stakeholders.
commonMistakes: Spending too much time perfecting a high-fidelity prototype before validating the core idea with a low-fidelity one.
Prototyping in isolation without getting feedback from real users, defeating its primary purpose.
